report_name: Europol Dismantles €336 Million 'AudiA6' Crypto Laundering Pipeline for Ransomware Gangs

threat_actors.name: AudiA6
threat_actors.description: A professional crypto mixing and laundering service that allegedly laundered over €336 million for ransomware gangs and other cybercriminals between 2022 and 2025. The service operated on the clear and dark web, using thousands of fraudulent accounts on cryptocurrency exchanges created with stolen or purchased identities.
threat_actors.goals: Cryptocurrency laundering for financial gain on behalf of other cybercriminals.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: The AudiA6 service used thousands of fraudulent accounts on cryptocurrency exchanges, created with stolen or purchased identities, to obscure the flow of illicit funds.

identities.name: Cryptocurrency Exchanges
identities.description: Class of financial services whose platforms were abused by the AudiA6 service, which created thousands of fraudulent accounts to conduct mixing and laundering operations.

infrastructure.name: AudiA6 Crypto Laundering Service
infrastructure.description: A major cryptocurrency laundering service operating on the clear and dark web, used to wash over €336 million for cybercriminals by mixing and tumbling funds.

infrastructure.name: Dark2Web Cybercrime Forum
infrastructure.description: A cybercrime forum to which the AudiA6 service was linked, likely for coordination and client acquisition.

infrastructure.name: AudiA6 Seized Servers
infrastructure.description: Over 30 servers seized by law enforcement that were part of the AudiA6 laundering infrastructure.
